title: Arthur Berti | Cyber Security Specialist
name: ARTHUR BERTI PETERSEN SCHOLZE
subtitle: TryHackMe Top 2% | Anthropic CVP | AUVP Capital
typewriter_text: Red Team & Pentest|RFID/NFC Hardware Hacking|Blue Team & Threat Hunting|MCP Developer
location: Southern Brazil — Rio Grande do Sul
summary_p1: Offensive and defensive cybersecurity professional working at AUVP Capital, based in Southern Brazil (RS).
summary_p2: Approved in Anthropic's Cybersecurity Verification Program (CVP) — a program for legitimate security professionals that tunes Claude's safeguards for dual-use work: vulnerability exploitation, payloads and offensive tooling.
summary_p3: Ranked in the Top 2% worldwide on TryHackMe and a speaker at UNICRUZ on the practical identification and analysis of security flaws in real-world environments.
summary_p4: Expertise in pentest, threat hunting and blue team, with strong specialization in hardware hacking (RFID/NFC) via Proxmark3 Iceman: analysis of MIFARE Classic, EMV (Mastercard M/Chip Advance — CDA, ARQC, AIP), iClass, T5577 and proprietary protocols.
summary_p5: Author of the Proxmark3 MCP Server — a Python wrapper that exposes ~56 Iceman client commands as MCP tools, integrating Proxmark3 directly with Claude over stdio JSON-RPC.
summary_p6: Fluent in Portuguese (native) and technical English. A self-taught professional who learns by doing, tests in his own lab and documents everything he produces — DOCX reports, presentations and technical analyses.
summary_p7: Core belief: AI in cybersec isn't the future — it's a daily tool. Heavy use of LLMs integrated with tooling via MCP in a real workflow.
cves_none: No vulnerabilities found with the selected filters.

exp3_desc: Working in cybersecurity for a technology company in Uruguay, I operated under strict professional secrecy (NDA). From the beginning, I was entrusted with the execution of critical tasks for the protection of sensitive systems and data, demonstrating a high degree of responsibility in the position.
exp5_desc: I developed a website in HTML (only the frontend part, as the backend was already ready)
exp6_desc: My start in the job market began here, I worked as a waiter, kitchen assistant, bartender and cashier manager. In short, I was a 'jack of all trades'
project1_desc: FastAPI-based security server with async support and comprehensive testing suite.
project2_desc: Security research and offensive tooling project.
project3_desc: Python wrapper exposing ~56 Iceman client commands as MCP tools — integrates Proxmark3 with Claude over stdio JSON-RPC. Supports MIFARE, EMV, T55xx, LF/HF and Lua/Python scripts.
contact_quote: "Security is not a product, but a process."
